story.iWant: to extract interfaces for EndorService and EndorHybridService
story.soThat: developers can mock these components in their tests
story.tasks: - [ ] Task 1: Create interfaces package and extract EndorServiceInterface (AC: 1, 2, 4) - [ ] Create `sdk/interfaces/` package directory - [ ] Create `sdk/interfaces/service.go` file - [ ] Analyze current EndorService methods and extract interface - [ ] Add comprehensive GoDoc documentation with usage examples - [ ] Include methods: GetResource(), GetDescription(), GetMethods(), and other public methods - [ ] Task 2: Extract EndorHybridServiceInterface (AC: 1, 2, 4) - [ ] Analyze current EndorHybridService methods and extract interface - [ ] Include hybrid-specific methods: WithCategories(), WithActions(), ToEndorService() - [ ] Add GoDoc documentation explaining hybrid service patterns - [ ] Ensure interface supports generic type parameters for type safety - [ ] Task 3: Implement interface compliance testing (AC: 3, 5) - [ ] Add compliance tests: `var _ EndorServiceInterface = (*EndorService)(nil)` - [ ] Add compliance tests: `var _ EndorHybridServiceInterface = (*EndorHybridService)(nil)` - [ ] Create unit tests demonstrating interface usage patterns - [ ] Verify all existing functionality works through interface contracts - [ ] Task 4: Update existing implementations (AC: 3) - [ ] Ensure EndorService concrete type satisfies EndorServiceInterface - [ ] Ensure EndorHybridService concrete type satisfies EndorHybridServiceInterface - [ ] Add any missing method implementations if interface extraction reveals gaps - [ ] Verify no breaking changes to existing public APIs

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. **Interface Definition** - EndorServiceInterface and EndorHybridServiceInterface are defined in `sdk/interfaces/service.go` 2. **Method Preservation** - All current method signatures are preserved in the interfaces 3. **Implementation Compliance** - Concrete implementations satisfy their respective interfaces 4. **Documentation** - Interfaces include comprehensive GoDoc documentation with examples 5. **Compliance Testing** - Interface compliance tests verify implementations satisfy interfaces

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: FR4: All framework components implement well-defined interfaces for easy mocking. Framework provides interface-based EndorService and EndorHybridService creation patterns.

doc.title: Refactored Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Interface Segregation with Smart Composition. Following Go's interface philosophy of "accept interfaces, return structs": EndorServiceInterface and EndorHybridServiceInterface for services, compose larger interfaces from smaller ones when needed.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Goal: Framework components become mockable and testable. User Value: Developers can write unit tests without external dependencies.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Interface Foundation & Testability
doc.snippet: EndorServiceInterface contract includes GetResource(), GetDescription(), GetMethods(), HandleRequest() methods. EndorHybridServiceInterface includes WithCategories(), WithActions(), ToEndorService(), GetSchema() methods.

artifact.reason: Current EndorService struct implementation that needs interface extraction
Current EndorHybridService interface and implementation for extraction
Implementation struct showing methods that need interface extraction
Example service creation pattern showing current EndorService usage
Existing test patterns showing current usage that will guide interface design

constraints.constraint: Follows Interface Segregation with Smart Composition pattern from architecture document
Implements "accept interfaces, return structs" Go philosophy
Uses Go 1.21+ generics for compile-time type safety where applicable
Maintains zero overhead abstractions with interface-based design
Must preserve all existing EndorService and EndorHybridService public API contracts
New interfaces enable mocking without changing concrete implementation behavior
Interface extraction is foundation for Epic 1's testability goals

interface.name: EndorServiceInterface
interface.signature: type EndorServiceInterface interface { GetResource() string GetDescription() string GetMethods() map[string]EndorServiceAction GetPriority() *int HandleRequest(ctx context.Context, req RequestInterface) (ResponseInterface, error) Validate() error }

interface.name: EndorHybridServiceInterface
interface.signature: type EndorHybridServiceInterface interface { GetResource() string GetResourceDescription() string GetPriority() *int WithCategories(categories []EndorHybridServiceCategory) EndorHybridService WithActions(fn func(getSchema func() RootSchema) map[string]EndorServiceAction) EndorHybridService ToEndorService(metadataSchema Schema) EndorService }

tests.standards: Go testing with testify framework. Interface compliance testing using var _ Interface = (*Implementation)(nil) pattern. Unit tests should use mocked dependencies through interfaces. Build tags separate unit tests from integration tests.
tests.locations: test/ directory for existing tests, sdk/interfaces/ for interface compliance tests

ideas.test: Test interface method signature extraction completeness
Interface compliance tests for EndorService and EndorHybridService implementations
Test GoDoc documentation examples are runnable
Unit tests demonstrating interface mocking patterns for both service types
